entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A hardy cereal crop (Pennisetum glaucum) grown in drylands

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Arid regions where pearl millet is primarily grown by smallholder farmers

entities.name: HARSH CLIMATIC CONDITIONS
entities.description: Extreme environmental conditions under which pearl millet can survive and grow

entities.name: SHELF-LIFE
entities.description: The duration for which pearl millet flour remains fit for consumption

entities.name: RANCIDITY
entities.description: The chemical decomposition of fats in pearl millet flour leading to unpleasant odors and tastes

entities.name: ACID VALUE
entities.description: A measurement parameter used to determine the level of enzymatic rancidity

entities.name: ENZYMATIC RANCIDITY
entities.description: A type of rancidity caused by the action of enzymes

entities.name: PEROXIDE VALUE
entities.description: A measurement parameter used to determine the level of oxidative rancidity

entities.name: OXIDATIVE RANCIDITY
entities.description: A type of rancidity caused by the oxidation of fats

entities.name: REFRIGERATED STORAGE
entities.description: A storage condition maintained at 4°C to evaluate rancidity

entities.name: ROOM TEMPERATURE STORAGE
entities.description: A storage condition maintained at 25°C to evaluate rancidity

entities.name: ACCELERATED STORAGE
entities.description: A storage condition maintained at 35°C and 70% relative humidity to evaluate rancidity

entities.name: SHELF-STABLE FLOUR
entities.description: Flour that is resistant to rancidity and has an extended shelf-life
